LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT 1049 MARKET STREET, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COURT MUST ENGAGE IN A TWO-STEP PROCESS WHEN RULING ON AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION UNDER CCP SEC. 425.16. THE FIRST PRONG IS MET IF THE MOVING PARTY MEETS THE BURDEN OF MAKING A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT THE PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION ARISES OUT OF AN ACT IN FURTHERANCE OF A PERSON'S RIGHT TO PETITION OR FREE SPEECH UNDER THE US OR CA CONST. IN CONNECTION WITH A PUBLIC ISSUE. (CCP SEC. 425.16(B)(1).) DEFENDANT HAS NOT MADE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT ANY OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT ARGUES THAT PLAINTIFFS FILED THIS SUIT IN ORDER TO CHILL ITS CONSTITUTIONALLY PROTECTED RIGHT TO PETITION, I.E. FILING EVICTION NOTICES, "[O]UR SUPREME COURT HAS MADE IT CLEAR THAT A PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT NECESSARILY ARISE FROM A DEFENDANT'S SECTION 425.16 PROTECTED ACTIVITY MERELY BECAUSE THE PLAINTIFF'S SUIT WAS FILED AFTER THE DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN THAT ACTIVITY." (OVIEDO V. WINDSOR TWELVE PROPERTIES, LLC (2012) 212 CAL.APP.4TH 97, 110.) RATHER, "THE DEFENDANT'S ACT UNDERLYING THE PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION MUST ITSELF HAVE BEEN AN ACT IN FURTHERANCE OF THE RIGHT OF PETITION OR FREE SPEECH." (ID.) HERE, ALL OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED FAILURE TO MAINTAIN THE HABITABILITY OF THE BUILDING IN AN EFFORT TO PRESSURE THE PLAINTIFFS TO VOLUNTARILY MOVE OUT. BECAUSE THE FIRST PRONG IS NOT MET HERE, THE COURT NEED NOT ANALYZE THE SECOND PRONG. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS NOT FRIVOLOUS AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE MOTION WAS FILED TO SOLELY CAUSE DELAY. (CCP SEC. 425.16(C)(1).) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

Hearing Of Defendant 1049 Market Street, Llc'S Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gregory Brod, Esq., 415.397.1130, for plaintiff Clarence Wilson et al.
Richard Diestel, Esq. and Tessa Weeks, Esq., 415.981.5411, for defendant 1049 Market Street, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendant 1049 Market Street, LLC'S Motion To Strike Complaint is denied. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an Anti-SLAPP motion under CCP sec. 425.16. The first prong is met if the moving party meets the burden of making a prima facie showing that the plaintiff's cause of action arises out of an act in furtherance of a person's right to petition or free speech under the US or CA Const. in connection with a public issue. (CCP sec. 425.16(b)(1).) Defendant has not made a prima facie showing that any of the causes of action arise from protected activity. Although Defendant argues that Plaintiffs filed this suit in order to chill its Constitutionally protected right to petition, i.e. filing eviction notices, "[o]ur Supreme Court has made it clear that a plaintiff's cause of action does not necessarily arise from a defendant's section 425.16 protected activity merely because the plaintiff's suit was filed after the defendant engaged in that activity." (Oviedo v. Windsor Twelve Properties, LLC (2012) 212 Cal.App.4th 97, 110.) Rather, "the defendant's act underlying the plaintiff's cause of action must itself have been an act in furtherance of the right of petition or free speech." (Id.) Here, all of the causes of action arise from Defendant's alleged failure to maintain the habitability of the building in an effort to pressure the Plaintiffs to voluntarily move out. Because the first prong is not met here, the Court need not analyze the second prong. Plaintiffs' request for attorneys' fees is denied. The motion is not frivolous and there is no evidence that the motion was filed to solely cause delay. (CCP sec. 425.16(c)(1).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
